betterquesting:10.desc:8:
§6Tank Modules§f

§aAdvanced modules for monitoring, interfacing, and automating your tanks!§f

The §9Pressure§f mod offers a set of add-on modules that bolt onto any Pressure tank to expose its fluid state to automation, redstone, and computer control. Up to §e10 modules§f can be installed per tank, allowing a single tank to be both filtered, drained, and redstone-controlled at the same time.

§6Tank Interface§f (pressure:tank_fluid_access)
Allows draining and filling containers. Works with §bhoppers§f, pipes from other mods, and conduits. Empty and full containers can be piped in and out from any side, making the tank a drop-in replacement for any standard fluid container.

§6Tank Fluid Filter§f (pressure:tank_filter)
Shows the current filter setting of the tank. Configure by §9right-clicking with a §bConfigurator§f§9§f. If a filter is set, only matching fluid is accepted by the tank — all other fluids are rejected on intake.

§6Tank Sensor§f (pressure:tank_sensor)
Checks conditions on the tank and emits a §bredstone signal§f when conditions are met. Up to §e10 sensors§f per tank. Signal is weak (won't pass through blocks) and emits from only one side. The GUI allows setting the §bsensor kind§f and §bparameters§f (e.g. fluid level thresholds, fluid type checks). Can be rotated with any wrench.

§6Tank Data Port§f (pressure:tank_data_port)
Only available if §bComputerCraft§f is installed. Connects directly to a computer or via wired modems. Provides a peripheral API for programmatic tank management (all calls take at least §e1 tick§f, and operations can be parallelized).

§eTip§f: Combine a §bTank Sensor§f set to a fluid-level threshold with a §bTank Fluid Filter§f to build a fully automatic fluid transfer setup that only fills a downstream machine when specific fluids are present at specific levels.
